channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: An Israeli strike on a Gaza humanitarian zone tent camp kills at least 40 people, Palestinians say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a crowded tent camp housing Palestinians displaced by the war in Gaza killed at least 40 people and wounded 60 others early Tuesday, Palestinian officials said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: During Brazil's worst drought, wildfires rage and the Amazon River falls to a record low
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — Brazil is enduring its worst drought since nationwide measurements began over seven decades ago, with 59% of the country under stress — an area roughly half the size of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A young golden eagle attacks a toddler in Norway, its fourth such assault on humans
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — At first, Francis Ari Sture thought a human was trying to shove him down the steep Norwegian mountainside. Then he saw the golden eagle land. “We are staring at each other for, maybe, a whole minute,” Sture told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syria says Israeli strikes kill 18 people in a large-scale attack
item.description: MASYAF, Syria (AP) — The number of people killed in overnight Israeli strikes in Syria has risen to 18 with dozens more wounded, Syria's health minister said Monday — the largest death toll in such an attack since the beginning of the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Flooding kills more than 20 people in Morocco and Algeria
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Torrential downpours hit North Africa's normally arid mountains and deserts over the weekend, causing flooding that killed nearly two dozen people in Morocco and Algeria and destroyed homes and critical infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A robot resumes mission to retrieve a piece of melted fuel from inside a damaged Fukushima reactor
item.description: An operation to send an extendable robot into one of three damaged reactors at Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant to bring back a tiny gravel of melted fuel debris has resumed, nearly three weeks after its earlier attempt was suspended due to a technical issue.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: International manhunt underway for man accused of committing ‘most cowardly’ crime
item.description: An international manhunt is underway for a man who doused a 9-month-old baby with scalding hot coffee at a park in Australia late last month, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Severe drought drops water level to historic low on the Paraguay River, a regional lifeline
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — A powerful drought in the Amazon rainforest led on Monday to the lowest water levels on the Paraguay River in more than a century, disrupting commerce on the major waterway, creating hazards for local transport and offering a grim warning for other parts of the world.
item.dc:creator: EMILIO SANABRIA and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: National police director, fugitive banker among 9 dead in El Salvador helicopter crash
item.description: El Salvador's police director, other top-ranking officials and fugitive banker Manuel Coto were reportedly killed in a military helicopter crash Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians' UN proposal demands Israel leave Gaza and the West Bank in 6 months
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The Palestinians have circulated a draft U.N. resolution demanding that Israel end its “unlawful presence” in Gaza and the West Bank within six months.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US halts plan to remove iconic stray cats from a historic area in Puerto Rico's capital
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The U.S. government has temporarily halted a plan to remove iconic stray cats that live in a historic district in Puerto Rico’s capital until a lawsuit opposing the project is resolved, a nonprofit announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Dutch court convicts 2 Pakistani men over death threats to anti-Islam lawmaker Geert Wilders
item.description: SCHIPHOL, Netherlands (AP) — A Dutch court convicted two Pakistani religious and political leaders in their absence Monday over calls to their followers to murder anti-Islam lawmaker Geert Wilders, the leader of the Party for Freedom that won last year’s general election in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iranian missiles in Russia are a legitimate target, a Ukrainian official says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A senior Ukrainian official said Monday Western partner countries must allow Ukraine to use weapons they have supplied to strike military warehouses inside Russia because of strong suspicions Iran has provided ballistic missiles for the Kremlin’s war effort.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Princess Kate completes chemotherapy, set to return to public duties
item.description: Kate, the princess of Wales, says she has completed chemotherapy and will return to some public duties in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN rights chief urges voters to beware 'strongman' politicians who 'throw glitter in our eyes'
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. human rights chief on Monday urged voters to be wary of “strongman” politicians and personalities "that throw glitter in our eyes” as more elections loom in a busy electoral calendar this year.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean truth commission says it found more evidence of forced adoptions in the 1980s
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean commission found evidence that women were pressured into giving away their infants for foreign adoptions after giving birth at government-funded facilities where thousands of people were confined and enslaved from the 1960s to the 1980s.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah relies on 'sophisticated' tunnel system backed by Iran, North Korea in fight against Israel
item.description: Hezbollah has developed a sophisticated tunnel system with the backing of Iran and North Korea to counter Israel while simultaneously gaining support across Lebanon, becoming Israel's greatest threat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Activists criticize high cost of Pope Francis' visit to East Timor, one of the poorest nations
item.description: DILI, East Timor (AP) — East Timor pulled out all stops for Pope Francis’ historic visit to one of the world’s youngest and poorest countries to the tune of $12 million, drawing rebuke from activists and human rights organizations in a nation where almost half the population lives in poverty.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis enters Papua New Guinea's remote jungles with humanitarian aid and toys
item.description: VANIMO, Papua New Guinea (AP) — Pope Francis traveled to the remote jungles of Papua New Guinea on Sunday to celebrate the Catholic Church on the peripheries, bringing with him medicine, musical instruments and a message of love for the people who live there.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Three Israeli civilians killed at crossing between West Bank and Jordan
item.description: Three Israeli civilians were killed on Sunday by a Jordanian gunman who opened fire at the Allenby Crossing connecting the West Bank and Jordan, the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels claim they shot down another US MQ-9 drone
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels claimed early Sunday they shot down another American-made MQ-9 drone flying over the country, marking potentially the latest downing of the multimillion-dollar surveillance aircraft. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opposition presidential candidate González flees Venezuela for asylum in Spain
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Former Venezuelan presidential candidate Edmundo González arrived in Spain on Sunday after fleeing into exile in as part of a negotiated deal with Nicolás Maduro's government that dealt a major blow to millions who placed their hopes in his opposition campaign.
item.dc:creator: JORGE RUEDA, JOSHUA GOODMAN and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two NATO members say Russian drones violated their airspace
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Two NATO members said Sunday that Russian drones violated their airspace, as one reportedly flew into Romania during nighttime attacks on neighboring Ukraine while another crashed in eastern Latvia the previous day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An inside look at Israel's special forces rescue unit fighting terrorists, rescuing hostages: 'Full on Fauda'
item.description: An inside look at Israel's elite Yamam counter-terrorism unit; their daring Gaza hostage rescue, the unit's secretive operations, and their critical role in the global fight against terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, British intel officials laud Ukraine for 'audacious and bold' Russian incursion
item.description: The heads of the American and British foreign intelligence agencies praised Ukraine’s incursion into Russia as Ukraine attempts to shift the course of the war.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Injured bear cub found on highway fed Taco Bell while in transit to receive treatment
item.description: A small black bear found injured in the middle of a Canadian highway was fed an American staple before being handed over to animal services.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump or Harris? Israelis discuss presidential choice as war with Hamas, other terror groups continues
item.description: Tensions run high as the U.S. presidential elections loom near. Israelis are concerned about what the next U.S. President would mean for their nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Zealand queen ascends to Māori throne at age 27
item.description: New Zealand’s Māori king, Tūheitia Pōtatau Te Wherowhero VII, 69, has died. His daughter, Ngā wai hono i te po, 27, is the new queen and the second woman to become Māori monarch.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli forces appear to withdraw from Jenin. But the operation may not be over
item.description: JENIN REFUGEE CAMP, West Bank (AP) — Israeli forces appeared to have withdrawn from three refugee camps in the occupied West Bank by Friday morning, after a more than weeklong military operation that left dozens dead and a trail of destruction.
item.dc:creator: JALAL BWAITEL and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American killed in West Bank, State Department says
item.description: An American citizen has died during a protest in the West Bank, Fox News has learned. The State Department says it is aware of "the tragic death" of Aysenur Ezgi Eygi, although did not provide any more details.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Underground wine city in Moldova owns nearly 2 million bottles, the world’s largest collection
item.description: Milestii Mici is the world's largest wine cellar. The cellar is underground, made up of a series of tunnels named after the wines stored there.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ticketmaster's pricing for Oasis tickets is under investigation in the UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s competition watchdog has launched an investigation into the way more than one million tickets were sold for next year's reunion concerts from iconic 1990s Britpop band Oasis.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A day after 12 deaths, other migrants risk their lives trying to cross the English Channel
item.description: That migrants were prepared to risk their lives so soon after a dozen others lost theirs trying to cross the busy waterway.
item.dc:creator: MARK CARLSON, NICOLAS GARRIGA and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas leaders charged with crimes by Justice Department
item.description: The Justice Department is charging top Hamas leaders for their role in the October 7th massacre in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Jessi Turnure, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-NBA star Enes Kanter Freedom on the cost of free speech
item.description: "Turkey was the bridge of Islam and the west. But just because of all this, Erdoğan's policies, that bridge is gone," he told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation
